followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use SLG1 application logging in BAdI implementations for incident diagnosis?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SLG1 application logging BAdI incident diagnosis mein ST22 dump analysis ko complement karta hai. Jabki ST22 sirf runtime dumps capture karta hai, SLG1 normal BAdI execution ke dauraan likhe custom log messages capture karta hai. BAdI implementations mein, main BAL_LOG_CREATE use karke custom application area jaise ZMM_PO_BADI ke saath log object initialize karta hoon, BAL_LOG_MSG_ADD use karke vendor number aur material number jaisi variable values include karte hue detailed log entries add karta hoon, aur BAL_DB_SAVE use karke logs persist karta hoon. Aisi escalation mein jahan koi dump occur nahi hua but wrong behavior report hua, main application area filter ke saath SLG1 check karke trace karta hoon ki BAdI ne exactly kya data process kiya aur kya decision liya. Yeh logic errors versus runtime errors diagnose karne ke liye invaluable hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SLG1 application logging complements ST22 dump analysis in BAdI incident diagnosis. While ST22 only captures runtime dumps, SLG1 captures custom log messages written during normal BAdI execution. In BAdI implementations, I initialize a log object using BAL_LOG_CREATE with a custom application area such as ZMM_PO_BADI, add detailed log entries using BAL_LOG_MSG_ADD including variable values like vendor number and material number, and persist logs using BAL_DB_SAVE. During an escalation where no dump occurred but wrong behavior was reported, I check SLG1 with the application area filter to trace exactly what data the BAdI processed and what decision it made. This is invaluable for diagnosing logic errors versus runtime errors.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Escalation aai: 'BAdI kuch vendors ke liye validation nahi kar rahi.' No ST22 dump. SLG1 check kiya — entries thi: LIFNR = '0000001234', MATNR = 'MAT001', result = 'Not found in catalog.' Lekin catalog mein entry thi. Pata chala: MATNR mein bhi leading zeros issue tha — same as LIFNR. SLG1 ne exact data values dikhaaye bina reproducing kiye. Without SLG1 it would have taken hours to debug.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: BAdI mein logging skip karna — 'production mein extra code slow karega.' SLG1 overhead minimal hai aur diagnosis value bahut zyada. Especially complex BAdIs mein logging mandatory honi chahiye. Log entries expiry set karo — 30 days — so old logs auto-delete hote hain.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SLG1 Application Logging in BAdI
DATA: lv_log_handle TYPE balloghndl.

" Initialize log
CALL FUNCTION 'BAL_LOG_CREATE'
  EXPORTING
    i_s_log = VALUE bal_s_log(
      object    = 'ZMM_PO_BADI'
      subobject = 'CATALOG_CHECK'
      aldate_del = sy-datum + 30 )
  IMPORTING
    e_log_handle = lv_log_handle.

" Add log entry with variable details
CALL FUNCTION 'BAL_LOG_MSG_ADD'
  EXPORTING
    i_log_handle = lv_log_handle
    i_s_msg = VALUE bal_s_msg(
      msgty = 'I'
      msgid = 'ZMM_CATALOG'
      msgno = '010'
      msgv1 = im_matnr
      msgv2 = lv_lifnr_conv ).

" Save to database
CALL FUNCTION 'BAL_DB_SAVE'
  EXPORTING i_log_handle = lv_log_handle.

" View: SLG1 → Object ZMM_PO_BADI → Subobject CATALOG_CHECK

enhancements-031.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: FS matlab Functional Specification — business analyst ya consultant likhta hai. Yeh document business language mein hota hai — 'kya karna hai.' TS matlab Technical Specification — developer likhta hai. Yeh document technical language mein hota hai — 'kaise karna hai.' Ek example: FS likhega: 'Jab sales order save ho, agar customer ka credit block X hai, toh warning dikhaao aur sales manager ko override karne do.' Yeh business requirement hai. Developer ko is FS ko padhke decide karna hai: kaunsa BAdI use karein, kaunse table check karein, kaunsa authorization check karein. TS likhega: 'BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT use karo SD module mein. KNB1 table se customer credit block field SPERR check karo. Agar X hai toh W type MESSAGE. Override ke liye SU01 mein custom authorization role check karo.' Yeh technical solution hai. FS to TS conversion matlab business requirement ko technical solution mein translate karna — developer ka main skill hai yeh. Agar tum FS padhke direct TS likh sako — BAdI select karo, table identify karo, field map karo — toh tum senior developer level pe kaam kar rahe ho.
enhancements-031.hinglishMasterExplanation:
FS to TS conversion sabse important skill hai jo interviews mein test hoti hai. Interviewer deta hai FS aur poochhhta hai — 'How would you technically implement this?' Yeh sochne ka process systematically samjho.

Step 1 — FS carefully padhna:
Har sentence mein do cheezein identify karo: trigger (kab hoga) aur action (kya hoga). Example FS: 'Jab sales order save ho [trigger], agar customer credit block = X [condition], toh warning dikhaao [action 1] aur sales manager ko override karne do [action 2].'

Step 2 — Trigger ko technical event mein convert karo:
'Sales order save' = VA01 transaction mein save button. Technical: kaunsa BAdI save event pe fire karta hai? SE18 mein search karo — BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT SD module mein. Method CHECK_ON_SAVE ya similar. Yeh tera entry point hai.

Step 3 — Condition ko table aur field mein convert karo:
'Customer credit block = X' — kaunsi table mein hai? KNB1 table — customer master company code level data. Field SPERR — credit block flag. Data dictionary SE11 mein dekho. TS likhega: 'SELECT SINGLE sperr FROM knb1 WHERE kunnr = [order customer] AND bukrs = [order company code].' Agar SPERR = 'X' — condition true.

Step 4 — Action ko technical implementation mein convert karo:
'Warning dikhaao' = MESSAGE type W — user dekh sakta hai but proceed bhi kar sakta hai. 'Sales manager override karne do' — authorization concept: custom authorization object create karo ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE. Sales manager role mein assign karo. BAdI mein AUTHORITY-CHECK karo. Agar authority hai toh allow, nahi hai toh error.

Step 5 — TS draft karo:
BAdI: BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT in SD
Table: KNB1
Field: SPERR
Logic: Check SPERR → W message → Authority check ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE → if authorized allow, else E message
Message class: ZSD_CREDIT_MSGS
Authorization object: ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE
Role updates: ZSD_SALES_MANAGER role — add ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE

Yeh TS conversion format interview mein gold hai. Technical decisions explain karo — 'KNB1 kyunki yeh company code level customer data hai, SPERR field specifically credit block indicator hai.' Reasoning dikhao, not just conclusions.
enhancements-031.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'When converting this FS to a technical spec, I would identify the trigger as the sales order save event in VA01, which maps to the BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT BAdI in the SD module. The credit block condition maps to table KNB1, field SPERR — if value is X, the block is active. For the warning, I implement a type W message which the user can acknowledge. For the sales manager override, I create a custom authorization object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE and use AUTHORITY-CHECK in the BAdI method — if the user has this authorization, override is allowed, otherwise a hard error prevents save.'

Hinglish tip: TS conversion mein specific table names aur field names bolo — KNB1, SPERR — yeh database knowledge dikhata hai. Aur authorization concept mention karna senior developer maturity dikhata hai — junior developer sirf message implement karta, senior developer authorization bhi sochta hai. Interview mein yeh differentiation bahut strong impression deta hai.
enhancements-031.codeExamples:
" FS to TS conversion — complete implementation
" FS: Credit block warning with sales manager override

" BAdI: BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT (SD module)
" SE19: ZCL_IMPL_CREDIT_BLOCK_CHECK

CLASS zcl_impl_credit_block_check DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_ex_badi_save_document.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_impl_credit_block_check IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_ex_badi_save_document~check_before_save.
    DATA: lv_sperr   TYPE knb1-sperr,
          lv_kunnr   TYPE knb1-kunnr,
          lv_bukrs   TYPE knb1-bukrs.

    " Get customer and company code from order header
    lv_kunnr = is_vbak-kunnr.
    lv_bukrs = is_vbak-bukrs.  " or derive from sales org

    " Check KNB1 for credit block
    SELECT SINGLE sperr FROM knb1
      WHERE kunnr = @lv_kunnr
        AND bukrs = @lv_bukrs
      INTO @lv_sperr.

    IF lv_sperr = 'X'.
      " Check if sales manager can override
      AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE'
        ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        " Has override authority — show warning only
        MESSAGE w001(zsd_credit_msgs)
          WITH lv_kunnr.
      ELSE.
        " No override authority — hard stop
        MESSAGE e002(zsd_credit_msgs)
          WITH lv_kunnr.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
enhancements-031.interviewScriptEnglish: When converting this FS to a technical spec, I would identify the trigger as the sales order save event in VA01, which maps to the BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT BAdI in the SD module. The credit block condition maps to table KNB1, field SPERR — if value is X, the block is active. For the warning, I implement a type W message which the user can acknowledge. For the sales manager override, I create a custom authorization object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE and use AUTHORITY-CHECK in the BAdI method — if the user has this authorization, override is allowed, otherwise a hard error prevents save.
enhancements-031.interviewScriptHinglish: is FS ko technical spec mein convert karte waqt main trigger ko VA01 ke sales order save event ke roop mein identify karunga, jo SD module mein BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT BAdI se map hota hai. Credit block condition table KNB1 ke field SPERR se map hoti hai — value X hai toh block active hai. Warning ke liye type W message implement karunga jise user acknowledge kar sake. Sales manager override ke liye custom authorization object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE banaunga aur BAdI method mein AUTHORITY-CHECK use karunga — agar user ke paas yeh authorization hai toh override allowed hai, warna hard error save prevent karega.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you decide which BAdI maps to a given FS trigger?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS trigger ko BAdI ke saath map karne ke liye: transaction aur exact process step identify karo — for example save time pe VA01. Module application area jaise SD sales ke liye SD-SLS aur event se match karne wale keywords jaise save ya check ke saath SE18 search karo. Candidate BAdIs ke short description aur method list review karo. Har candidate ke liye, method parameters check karo — kya method condition check ke liye needed data receive karta hai? Credit block example ke liye, mujhe customer number aur company code chahiye, isliye BAdI method ke paas order header data ka access hona chahiye. Timing cross-check karo: pre-save BAdIs save block karne dete hain, post-save BAdIs use prevent nahi kar sakti.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To map an FS trigger to a BAdI: identify the transaction and the exact process step — for example VA01 at save time. Search SE18 with the module application area such as SD-SLS for SD sales, and keywords matching the event such as save or check. Review the short description and method list of candidate BAdIs. For each candidate, check the method parameters — does the method receive the data needed for the condition check? For the credit block example, I need the customer number and company code, so the BAdI method must have access to order header data. Cross-check timing: pre-save BAdIs allow blocking the save, post-save BAdIs cannot prevent it.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT ke alawa BADI_SD_SALES_DOC_CHECK bhi option tha. Difference: CHECK method parameters mein — BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT mein complete header structure milti thi including BUKRS, BADI_SD_SALES_DOC_CHECK mein item level data better tha. Requirement header check tha — isliye BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT chose kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: First BAdI name dekha aur decide kar liya bina method parameters check kiye. Phir implement kiya aur pata chala ki required data available nahi hai method mein. Time waste. Always verify parameters in SE18 before committing to a BAdI.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAdI selection process — systematic approach
" Step 1: Identify trigger
"   'Sales order save' → Transaction VA01 → Save event
" Step 2: SE18 search
"   Application area: SD-SLS
"   Short description contains: 'save' OR 'check'
" Step 3: Candidate evaluation
"   BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT → CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE method
"     Parameters: IS_VBAK (header), IT_VBAP (items)
"     IS_VBAK has: KUNNR, BUKRS → PERFECT
"   BADI_SD_SALES_DOC_CHECK → CHECK method
"     Parameters: item-focused
"     Header data: less complete → NOT IDEAL
" Step 4: Select BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT
"   Reasoning: Has KUNNR + BUKRS, pre-save timing

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you map FS business data to technical SAP tables?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS business terms ko technical tables ke saath map karna kuch techniques involve karta hai. Standard business data ke liye, common tables ka knowledge essential hai: customer data KNA1 aur KNB1 mein, vendor data LFA1 aur LFB1 mein, material data MARA aur MARC mein, sales order header VBAK mein aur items VBAP mein. Unknown terms ke liye, description keyword se SE11 table search relevant tables find karne mein help karta hai. SE16N data browser table mein kya data exist karta hai dikhata hai. Standard transaction debug karte time work area variables dekhna exactly dikhata hai ki SAP same data ke liye kaunsi tables use karta hai. New areas ke liye, SAP Community aur official data model documentation reliable references hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Mapping FS business terms to technical tables involves several techniques. For standard business data, knowledge of common tables is essential: customer data is in KNA1 and KNB1, vendor data in LFA1 and LFB1, material data in MARA and MARC, sales order header in VBAK and items in VBAP. For unknown terms, SE11 table search by description keyword helps find relevant tables. SE16N data browser shows what data exists in a table. Debugging the standard transaction while watching work area variables reveals exactly which tables SAP uses for the same data. For new areas, SAP Community and the official data model documentation are reliable references.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: 'Customer credit block' — FS mein sirf yeh likha tha. Maine socha: credit data kahan hoti hai? Customer company code level data = KNB1. Field list dekhi SE11 mein — SPERR field tha with description 'Posting block.' Documentation confirm kiya: SPERR = X means credit block. Table-field mapping 10 minutes mein confirm ho gayi.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Table name guess karna bina verification ke. 'Credit blocking aata hoga KNA1 mein' — actually KNB1 mein hai (company code level). Always verify in SE11 or SE16N before coding.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document TS so the next developer can understand it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Accha Technical Specification document contain karta hai: FS requirement number ka reference, selection ke justification ke saath selected BAdI name, har kyun chosen tha uska explanation ke saath use kiye SAP tables aur fields, code logic ke parallel plain language mein described business logic, banaye gaye custom objects jaise authorization objects aur message classes apne purpose ke saath, happy path, error cases, aur edge cases cover karte test scenarios, aur known limitations ya assumptions. Code mein, har method ke paas function comment block aur kyun explain karne wale inline comments hone chahiye — sirf kya nahi. Good documentation ka test yeh hai: kya project ka part na rahne wala developer 2 years baad bina questions puchhe is code ko maintain kar sakta hai?
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A good Technical Specification document contains: reference to the FS requirement number, selected BAdI name with justification for selection, SAP tables and fields used with explanation of why each was chosen, business logic described in plain language parallel to the code logic, custom objects created such as authorization objects and message classes with their purpose, test scenarios covering happy path, error cases, and edge cases, and known limitations or assumptions. In code, every method should have a function comment block and inline comments explaining why — not just what. The test for good documentation is: can a developer who was not part of the project maintain this code 2 years later without asking questions?
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project pe ek undocumented BAdI mila — code tha, koi comment nahi, koi TS nahi. 2 din lage samajhne mein ki yeh kya kar raha hai. Client ko bola: 'This is technical debt, let me document it.' Spent 1 day writing proper TS. Next time someone needed to modify it: 2 hours instead of 2 days.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS likhna ya code commenting skip karna time pressure mein. 'Main hi maintain karoonga' attitude. Projects change — team members leave, someone else inherits the code. Documentation is professional responsibility.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
"*--------------------------------------------------------------*
" Class: ZCL_IMPL_CREDIT_BLOCK_CHECK
" BAdI: BADI_SAVE_DOCUMENT
" FS Reference: FS-SD-042 Rev 2.1
" Author: Developer Name | Date: DD.MM.YYYY
" Purpose: Credit block warning with manager override
"*--------------------------------------------------------------*
METHOD if_ex_badi_save_document~check_before_save.
  " Get customer credit block from KNB1
  " KNB1 = Customer master company code data
  " SPERR = 'X' means credit block is active
  SELECT SINGLE sperr FROM knb1
    WHERE kunnr = @lv_kunnr
      AND bukrs = @lv_bukrs
    INTO @lv_sperr.

  IF lv_sperr = 'X'.
    " Check override authority for sales managers
    " Auth object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE defined in SU21
    " Assigned to role ZSD_SALES_MANAGER in PFCG
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE'
      ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.
    " SY-SUBRC = 0: has authority → W message only
    " SY-SUBRC <> 0: no authority → E message, block save
  ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the MESSAGE statement syntax and types used in BAdI implementations?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: BAdI implementations mein, MESSAGE statement types most commonly used hain W Warning ke liye jo yellow message display karta hai jise user acknowledge karke proceed kar sakta hai, aur E Error ke liye jo hard stop create karta hai current operation ko complete hone se prevent karte hue. Syntax MESSAGE hai phir type letter, message number, parentheses mein message class, aur optionally WITH up to four variables jo message text mein ampersand placeholders replace karte hain. Message classes SE91 mein create hote hain aur transportable objects hain. BAdI context mein, check method mein E type message raise karna effectively business document ko save hone se prevent karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In BAdI implementations, the MESSAGE statement types most commonly used are W for Warning which displays a yellow message the user can acknowledge and proceed, and E for Error which creates a hard stop preventing the current operation from completing. The syntax is MESSAGE followed by the type letter, the message number, the message class in parentheses, and optionally WITH up to four variables that replace ampersand placeholders in the message text. Message classes are created in SE91 and are transportable objects. In BAdI context, raising an E type message in a check method effectively prevents the business document from being saved.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: UAT mein bada debate tha: W ya E type. Business team: 'W use karo, users ko decide karne do.' Manager: 'Users W ignore karte hain, E chahiye.' Final decision: two-phase — first week W, monitor how many users proceed despite warning. If >10% ignore it, convert to E. Result: 45% ignoring W — converted to E. Data-driven decision.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sabke liye E type message use karna — user experience poor hota hai. Ya W type use karna jab hard business rule hai. Message type = business decision, not just technical. Discuss with business analyst.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" MESSAGE types in BAdI
" Syntax: MESSAGE type+number(class) WITH var1 var2.

" Warning — user can proceed after acknowledging
MESSAGE w001(zsd_credit_msgs)
  WITH lv_kunnr.           " W001: Customer & has credit block

" Error — hard stop, save blocked
MESSAGE e002(zsd_credit_msgs)
  WITH lv_kunnr lv_bukrs.  " E002: Credit block & company & prevents save

" Success — confirmation message
MESSAGE s003(zsd_credit_msgs). " S003: Override approved

" Message class ZSD_CREDIT_MSGS (SE91):
" 001: Customer & has an active credit block (W)
" 002: Save blocked: Customer & in company & has credit block (E)
" 003: Credit block override authorized for this user (S)
" 004: Credit block check bypassed by manager override (I)

" Storing message text for log:
MESSAGE e002(zsd_credit_msgs)
  WITH lv_kunnr lv_bukrs INTO DATA(lv_msg).

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle the authorization object creation for BAdI override scenarios?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: BAdI override ke liye custom authorization object banana: SU21 mein, authorization object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE banao, ise ek authorization class ke saath assign karo, aur standard activity type domain use karke field ACTVT add karo. Optionally company code restriction ke liye BUKRS add karo. PFCG mein, sales manager role open karo aur authorization object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE ko execute ya create ke liye ACTVT value 01 ke saath add karo. SU01 ke through ya PFCG ke through mass assignment ke through role ko sales manager user IDs ke saath assign karo. BAdI code mein, AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE' ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01' use karo — SY-SUBRC 0 matlab authorized, non-zero matlab not authorized.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Creating a custom authorization object for BAdI override: in SU21, create authorization object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE, assign it to an authorization class, and add field ACTVT using the standard activity type domain. Optionally add BUKRS for company code restriction. In PFCG, open the sales manager role and add authorization object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE with ACTVT value 01 for execute or create. Assign the role to sales manager user IDs via SU01 or mass assignment via PFCG. In the BAdI code, use AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE' ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01' — SY-SUBRC 0 means authorized, non-zero means not authorized.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SU24 bhi update karna zaroori tha — transaction VA01 mein new authorization object ka proposal add kiya taaki future roles correctly generated ho sakein. Yeh step bhool jaate hain junior developers — SU24 update karna best practice hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Custom authorization object banana lekin SU24 mein VA01 ke liye proposal nahi add karna. Phir role generation mein object miss ho jaata hai. Ek aur: hardcoding user name check karna instead of authority-check — 'IF sy-uname = MANAGER01' — yeh terrible practice hai, never do this.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SU21: Authorization object creation
" Object: ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE
" Class: SD (or custom ZCSD)
" Fields:
"   ACTVT — Activity (domain ACTVT)
"   BUKRS — Company Code (optional, domain BUKRS)

" PFCG: Role ZSD_SALES_MANAGER
"   → Authorization tab → Add object ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE
"   → ACTVT: 01  BUKRS: 1000 (or *)

" BAdI code: Authority check
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE'
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs.  " company code specific

IF sy-subrc = 0.
  " Authorized sales manager — warning only
  MESSAGE w001(zsd_credit_msgs) WITH lv_kunnr.
ELSE.
  " Not authorized — hard error
  MESSAGE e002(zsd_credit_msgs) WITH lv_kunnr.
ENDIF.

" SU24: Add proposal for VA01
" SU24 → VA01 → Add ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE → Check = 1

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test the FS requirement coverage in your TS implementation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS coverage testing ke liye traceability matrix chahiye: har FS requirement bullet point ek ya zyada test cases ke saath map karta hai. Credit block FS ke liye: requirement ek — warning dikhao — test case TC001 normal user, blocked customer ke saath map karta hai, warning aur save succeeds expected. Requirement do — sales manager override kar sakta hai — TC002 sales manager role user, blocked customer ke saath map karta hai, sirf warning aur save succeeds expected. Requirement teen — normal user override nahi kar sakta — TC003 normal user, blocked customer ke saath map karta hai, error aur save blocked expected. Requirements char onwards — unblocked customers ke liye no block — TC004 ke saath map karta hai. Har test case ke paas expected result, actual result, aur pass ya fail status hota hai. UAT sign-off sirf tab milta hai jab sab test cases pass hon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing FS coverage requires a traceability matrix: each FS requirement bullet point maps to one or more test cases. For the credit block FS: requirement one — show warning — maps to test case TC001 normal user, blocked customer, expects warning and save succeeds. Requirement two — sales manager can override — maps to TC002 sales manager role user, blocked customer, expects warning only and save succeeds. Requirement three — normal user cannot override — maps to TC003 normal user, blocked customer, expects error and save blocked. Requirements four onwards — no block for unblocked customers — maps to TC004. Each test case has expected result, actual result, and pass or fail status. UAT sign-off is only given when all test cases pass.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein 'sales manager override' likha tha lekin define nahi kiya tha ki 'sales manager' kaun hai — role name kya hai? Gap: FS clarification needed before TS. Asked BA: 'Which SAP role represents sales manager?' Got answer: ZSD_SALES_MANAGER. Always resolve ambiguity in FS before writing TS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assumption-based implementation — FS clear nahi tha aur developer ne assume kiya. Implementation galat ho gayi. FS mein koi bhi ambiguity ho toh BA se clarify karo pehle, implement baad mein. Document the clarification too.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the KNB1 table and why is it used for credit block check?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: KNB1 SAP mein Customer Master Company Code Data table hai, jo customer ke liye har company code specific data store karta hai. SAP mein customer master data teen levels mein organized hai: name aur address jaise general client-level data ke liye KNA1, reconciliation account, payment terms, credit limit KLIMK, aur credit block indicator SPERR jaise company code specific data ke liye KNB1, aur sales area specific data ke liye KNVV. Credit block SPERR KNB1 mein rehta hai kyunki credit management company code level pe handle hota hai — different company codes same customer ke liye different credit standings rakh sakte hain. BAdI mein, main sales order se KUNNR customer number aur BUKRS company code dono use karke KNB1 read karta hoon relevant company code ke liye correct credit block status get karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: KNB1 is the Customer Master Company Code Data table in SAP, storing data that is specific to each company code for a customer. Customer master data in SAP is organized in three levels: KNA1 for general client-level data such as name and address, KNB1 for company code specific data such as reconciliation account, payment terms, credit limit KLIMK, and credit block indicator SPERR, and KNVV for sales area specific data. The credit block SPERR lives in KNB1 because credit management is handled at company code level — different company codes may have different credit standings for the same customer. In the BAdI, I read KNB1 using both KUNNR customer number and BUKRS company code from the sales order to get the correct credit block status for the relevant company code.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Junior developer ek baar KNA1 se credit block check karne ki koshish kar raha tha. KNA1 mein SPERR field hai nahi — wrong table. Samjhaya: 'credit is financial, company code level, isliye KNB1.' Table hierarchy samajhna important hai — KNA1, KNB1, KNVV, KNVP relationships.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: KNA1 aur KNB1 confuse karna — common mistake. KNA1 mein general data hai, KNB1 mein financial/company-level data hai. Rule of thumb: kuch bhi financial ya company-code specific hai toh KNB1. Kuch bhi general customer info hai toh KNA1.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Customer master table hierarchy
" KNA1: Client level (one record per customer)
"   KUNNR, NAME1, ORT01, LAND1, STCD1 (tax number)

" KNB1: Company code level (one per customer+bukrs)
"   KUNNR + BUKRS = Key
"   SPERR = Credit/posting block ('X' = blocked)
"   KLIMK = Credit limit (amount)
"   ZTERM = Payment terms
"   AKONT = Reconciliation GL account

" KNVV: Sales area level (one per customer+sales area)
"   KUNNR + VKORG + VTWEG + SPART = Key
"   KDGRP = Customer group
"   KONDA = Price group

" Credit block check — always KNB1:
SELECT SINGLE sperr klimk FROM knb1
  WHERE kunnr = @lv_kunnr
    AND bukrs = @lv_bukrs
  INTO ( @lv_sperr, @lv_klimk ).
" Both credit block flag AND credit limit in one select

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle multiple conditions in FS to TS conversion?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab FS mein multiple conditions hon, pehle determine karo ki conditions AND logic hain — sab true hone chahiye — ya OR logic hain — koi bhi ek action trigger karta hai. Agar FS kahe 'jab credit block X hai AND order value limit se zyada hai,' dono conditions simultaneously true hone chahiye. Har condition ko apne technical source ke saath map karo: KNB1-SPERR se credit block, KNB1-KLIMK ke against compared VBAK-NETWR se order value. Code mein, readability ke liye intermediate boolean variables ya DATA lv_credit_blocked TYPE abap_bool use karo. Deeply nested IF statements avoid karo — cleaner logic ke liye early RETURN pattern use karo. Har condition ko apne FS reference number ke saath document karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When FS has multiple conditions, first determine whether conditions are AND logic — all must be true — or OR logic — any one triggers the action. If the FS says 'when credit block is X AND order value exceeds limit,' both conditions must be true simultaneously. Map each condition to its technical source: credit block from KNB1-SPERR, order value from VBAK-NETWR compared against KNB1-KLIMK. In code, use intermediate boolean variables or DATA lv_credit_blocked TYPE abap_bool for readability. Avoid deeply nested IF statements — use early RETURN pattern for cleaner logic. Document each condition with its FS reference number.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein likha tha: 'credit block OR credit limit exceeded.' Developer ne AND implement kiya — wrong. UAT mein caught: customer had credit limit exceeded but no block flag — no warning shown. Bug: OR vs AND. FS ambiguity: 'AND/OR' clearly define karna chahiye. Clarification: when either condition is true, show warning.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AND vs OR assume karna FS se clarification ke bina. 'Aur' word Hindi mein AND aur OR dono ke liye use hota hai — always clarify with BA/client. Document the clarification email for audit.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Multiple conditions from FS:
" 'IF credit block = X OR order value > credit limit'

DATA: lv_credit_blocked  TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false,
      lv_limit_exceeded   TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

" Condition 1: credit block flag
SELECT SINGLE sperr klimk FROM knb1
  WHERE kunnr = @lv_kunnr AND bukrs = @lv_bukrs
  INTO ( @lv_sperr, @lv_klimk ).

IF lv_sperr = 'X'.
  lv_credit_blocked = abap_true.
ENDIF.

" Condition 2: order value vs credit limit
IF is_vbak-netwr > lv_klimk.
  lv_limit_exceeded = abap_true.
ENDIF.

" OR logic: either condition triggers warning
IF lv_credit_blocked = abap_true
OR lv_limit_exceeded = abap_true.
  " Check override authority and show message
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZSD_CREDIT_OVERRIDE'
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    MESSAGE w001(zsd_credit_msgs) WITH lv_kunnr.
  ELSE.
    MESSAGE e002(zsd_credit_msgs) WITH lv_kunnr.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.
